This is the optimal approach\n80\t    for hot-path LLM response parsing.\n81\t\n82\t    Args:\n83\t        raw: Raw response text that may contain markdown fences\n84\t\n85\t    Returns:\n86\t        Text with markdown fences removed\n87\t\n88\t    Examples:\n89\t        >>> strip_markdown_fences('```json\\\\n{\"a\": 1}\\\\n```')\n90\t        '{\"a\": 1}'\n91\t        >>> strip_markdown_fences('```\\\\n{\"a\": 1}\\\\n```')\n92\t        '{\"a\": 1}'\n93\t        >>> strip_markdown_fences('{\"a\": 1}')\n94\t        '{\"a\": 1}'\n95\t    \"\"\"\n96\t    # Stripping markdown code fences (```json ... ```)\n179\t    2. Parsing JSON (when expect_json=True)\n180\t    3. Providing clear error messages\n181\t\n182\t    Performance: Optimized with fast manual fence stripping (7-179x faster than\n183\t    regex) and early returns on empty inputs. Centralized utility for parsing LLM responses.\n\nHandles:\n1. ZAI proxy response unwrapping (removes outer \"result\" field)\n2. Markdown code fence stripping (```json, ```, etc.)\n3. JSON parsing with clear error messages\n4. Graceful fallback for malformed responses\n\nThis utility is used across:\n- Intent Router (src/intent/router.py)\n- Synthesize Strand (src/synthesize/strand.py)\n- Self-Modification Agent (src/agents/self_modification.py)\n- Any other LLM interaction points\n\n## Error Handling Patterns\n\nWhen ParseLLMError is raised, choose recovery strategy based on context:\n\n### Corrective Retry Pattern (Router-style)\nUse when:\n- LLM call is early in pipeline (cheap to retry)\n- Failure cascades to downstream operations\n- Retrying doesn't require re-running expensive operations\n\nExample: Intent router retries once because malformed response affects all downstream processing.\n\nSee: src/intent/router.py classify_utterance()\n\n### Fallback Result Pattern (Synthesize-style)\nUse when:\n- LLM call is AFTER expensive operations (fetch, DB queries, etc.)\n- Data already obtained should not be discarded\n- User should see partial results in degraded-state UX\n\nExample: Synthesize strand returns fallback result because fetch operations already completed.\n\nSee: src/synthesize/strand.py synthesize()\n\n### Documentation\nSee: docs/error-handling-standardization.md for complete pattern comparison and examples.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport json\nfrom logging import getLogger\nfrom typing import Any, Dict, Optional, cast\n\nlogger = getLogger(__name__)\n\n\nclass ParseLLMError(Exception):\n    \"\"\"\n    Raised when LLM response parsing fails.\n\n    Attributes:\n        message: Human-readable error message\n        raw_response: The raw LLM response text that failed parsing (preserved for debugging)\n\n    The raw_response attribute is critical for debugging and degraded-state UX.\n    Always pass it when raising ParseLLMError so error handlers can include\n    response snippets in error events and logs.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, message: str, raw_response: Optional[str] = None):\n        self.raw_response = raw_response\n        super().__init__(message)\n\n\ndef strip_markdown_fences(raw: str) -> str:\n    \"\"\"\n    Strip markdown code fences from a response using fast manual string splitting.\n\n    Handles:\n    - ```json ... ```\n    - ``` ... ```\n    - ` ... ` (rare but possible)\n\n    Performance: Uses manual string splitting which is 7-179x faster than regex-based\n    alternatives (see benchmarking in notes/adc-3e5gg.md). This is the optimal approach\n    for hot-path LLM response parsing.\n\n    Args:\n        raw: Raw response text that may contain markdown fences\n\n    Returns:\n        Text with markdown fences removed\n\n    Examples:\n        >>> strip_markdown_fences('```json\\\\n{\"a\": 1}\\\\n```')\n        '{\"a\": 1}'\n        >>> strip_markdown_fences('```\\\\n{\"a\": 1}\\\\n```')\n        '{\"a\": 1}'\n        >>> strip_markdown_fences('{\"a\": 1}')\n        '{\"a\": 1}'\n    \"\"\"\n    # Early return for empty/whitespace strings\n    if not raw or not raw.strip():\n        return raw\n\n    # Optimized: single strip at start, avoid redundant operations\n    text = raw.strip()\n\n    # Fast manual fence stripping (7-179x faster than regex)\n    # Pattern: ```optional_lang\\n content \\n```\n    if text.startswith(\"```\"):\n        # Split after first newline to skip opening fence line\n        text = text.split(\"\\n\", 1)[-1]\n        # Remove closing fence and any trailing whitespace\n        text = text.rsplit(\"```\", 1)[0].strip()\n\n    return text\n\n\ndef unwrap_zai_response(response_data: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Unwrap ZAI proxy response structure.\n\n    The ZAI proxy wraps Anthropic responses under a \"result\" key.\n    This function extracts the inner payload if present.\n\n    Args:\n        response_data: Raw response dict from ZAI proxy\n\n    Returns:\n        Unwrapped response dict (or original if no wrapping detected)\n\n    Examples:\n        >>> unwrap_zai_response({\"result\": {\"content\": [...]}})\n        {\"content\": [...]}\n        >>> unwrap_zai_response({\"content\": [...]})\n        {\"content\": [...]}\n    \"\"\"\n    # Check if response has \"result\" wrapper (ZAI proxy pattern)\n    if \"result\" in response_data and isinstance(response_data[\"result\"], dict):\n        inner = response_data[\"result\"]\n        logger.debug(\"Unwrapped ZAI proxy response (removed 'result' field)\")\n        return inner\n\n    return response_data\n\n\ndef extract_text_from_response(payload: Dict[str, Any]) -> str:\n    \"\"\"\n    Extract text content from an Anthropic-style response payload.\n\n    Args:\n        payload: Response payload with \"content\" array\n\n    Returns:\n        Extracted text content\n\n    Raises:\n        ParseLLMError: If content cannot be extracted\n    \"\"\"\n    content = payload.get(\"content\", [])\n\n    if content and isinstance(content, list) and len(content) > 0:\n        text = cast(str, content[0].get(\"text\", \"\"))\n        if text:\n            return text\n\n    # Fallback: try to stringify the content\n    logger.warning(\"Could not extract text from content array, using fallback\")\n    return str(content)\n\n\ndef parse_llm_response(\n    raw_text: str,\n    *,\n    strip_fences: bool = True,\n    expect_json: bool = True,\n) -> Any:\n    \"\"\"\n    Parse an LLM text response, optionally stripping fences and parsing JSON.\n\n    This is the main entry point for parsing LLM responses across the codebase.\n    It handles the common pattern of:\n    1. Stripping markdown code fences (```json ... ```)\n    2. Parsing JSON (when expect_json=True)\n    3. Providing clear error messages\n\n    Performance: Optimized with fast manual fence stripping (7-179x faster than\n    regex) and early returns on empty inputs. Operations are sequential but efficient.\n\n    Args:\n        raw_text: Raw response text from LLM\n        strip_fences: Whether to strip markdown code fences (default: True)\n        expect_json: Whether to parse as JSON (default: True)\n\n    Returns:\n        Parsed JSON object if expect_json=True, otherwise cleaned text\n\n    Raises:\n        ParseLLMError: If parsing fails\n\n    Examples:\n        >>> parse_llm_response('```json\\\\n{\"a\": 1}\\\\n```')\n        {'a': 1}\n        >>> parse_llm_response('```json\\\\n{\"a\": 1}\\\\n```', expect_json=False)\n        '{\"a\": 1}'\n    \"\"\"\n    # Early return for empty input\n    if not raw_text or not raw_text.strip():\n        if expect_json:\n            raise ParseLLMError(\n                \"Empty response provided\",\n                raw_response=raw_text,\n            )\n        return raw_text\n\n    try:\n        # Step 1: Strip markdown fences if present\n        if strip_fences:\n            text = strip_markdown_fences(raw_text)\n        else:\n            text = raw_text.strip()\n\n        # Step 2: Parse JSON if expected\n        if expect_json:\n            try:\n                return json.loads(text)\n            except json.JSONDecodeError as e:\n                # Provide helpful error message with snippet\n                snippet = text[:200] if text else \"(empty)\"\n                raise ParseLLMError(\n                    f\"Failed to parse JSON: {e}\\nResponse snippet: {snippet}...\",\n                    raw_response=raw_text,\n                ) from e\n\n        return text\n\n    except ParseLLMError:\n        # Re-raise ParseLLMError without wrapping\n        raise\n    except Exception as e:\n        raise ParseLLMError(\n            f\"Unexpected error parsing response: {e}\",\n            raw_response=raw_text,\n        ) from e\n\n\ndef parse_zai_proxy_response(\n    response_dict: Dict[str, Any],\n    *,\n    extract_text: bool = True,\n    strip_fences: bool = True,\n    expect_json: bool = True,\n) -> Any:\n    \"\"\"\n    Parse a complete ZAI proxy response from HTTP.\n\n    This handles the full flow:\n    1. Unwrap ZAI proxy's \"result\" field\n    2. Extract text from Anthropic content array\n    3. Strip markdown fences\n    4. Parse JSON if expected\n\n    Args:\n        response_dict: Raw response dict from httpx.Response.json()\n        extract_text: Whether to extract text from content array (default: True)\n        strip_fences: Whether to strip markdown fences (default: True)\n        expect_json: Whether to parse as JSON (default: True)\n\n    Returns:\n        Parsed JSON object or text depending on expect_json\n\n    Raises:\n        ParseLLMError: If parsing fails\n\n    Examples:\n        >>> response = {\"result\": {\"content\": [{\"text\": '```json\\\\n{\"a\": 1}\\\\n```'}]}}\n        >>> parse_zai_proxy_response(response)\n        {'a': 1}\n    \"\"\"\n    try:\n        # Step 1: Unwrap ZAI proxy response\n        unwrapped = unwrap_zai_response(response_dict)\n\n        # Step 2: Extract text from content structure\n        if extract_text:\n            text = extract_text_from_response(unwrapped)\n        else:\n            # Return the unwrapped payload as-is\n            return unwrapped\n\n        # Step 3 & 4: Strip fences and parse JSON using the main parser\n        return parse_llm_response(\n            text,\n            strip_fences=strip_fences,\n            expect_json=expect_json,\n        )\n\n    except ParseLLMError:\n        raise\n    except Exception as e:\n        raise ParseLLMError(\n            f\"Failed to parse ZAI proxy response: {e}\",\n            raw_response=str(response_dict)[:500],\n        ) from e\n","numLines":300,"startLine":1,"totalLines":300}}}

  self._client: Optional[httpx.AsyncClient] = None\n\n    async def _get_client(self) -> httpx.AsyncClient:\n        \"\"\"Get or create HTTP client.\"\"\"\n        if self._client is None:\n out\n            LLMRateLimitError: If rate limited\n            LLMError: For other errors\n        \"\"\"\n        client = await self._get_client()\n\n        try:\n            payload =      self.proxy_url,\n                json=payload,\n                headers={\n                    \"Content-Type\": \"application/json\",\n                },\n            )\n\n            # Check       content=text,\n                model=payload.get(\"model\", request.model),\n                input_tokens=input_tokens,\n                output_tokens=output_tokens,\n     except httpx.TimeoutException as e:\n            raise LLMTimeoutError(f\"LLM request timed out: {e}\") from e\n        except httpx.HTTPStatusError as e:\n            if e.response.status_code == 429:\n                raise LLMRateLimitError(\"Rate limited by ZAI proxy\") from e\n            raise LLMError(f\"LLM request failed: {e.response.status_code}\") from e\n        except (LLMTimeoutError, LLMRateLimitError, LLMError):\n            # Re-raise our own exceptions without wrapping\n            raise\n        except Exception as e:\n            raise LLMError(f\"LLM request failed: {e}\") from e\n\n    async def call_simple(\n        self,\n        system_prompt: str,\n        user_message: str,\n        model: str | None = None,\n        max_tokens: int = 4096,\n        temperature: float = 0.7,\n    ) -> str:\n        \"\"\"\n        Convenience method for simple LLM calls.\n\n        Returns just the text content.\n        \"\"\"\n        request = LLMRequest(\n            system_prompt=system_prompt,\n            user_message=user_message,\n            model=model or self.default_model,\n            max_tokens=max_tokens,\n            temperature=temperature,\n        )\n        response = await self.call(request)\n        return response.content\n\n    async def call_streaming(\n        self,\n        system_prompt: str,\n        user_message: str,\n        model: str | None = None,\n        max_tokens: int = 4096,\n        temperature: float = 0.7,\n    ) -> AsyncGenerator[Union[str, Dict[str, Any]], None]:\n        \"\"\"\n        Stream LLM response for progressive card fill.\n\n        Yields text chunks as they arrive from the API.\n\n        Args:\n            system_prompt: System prompt for the LLM\n            user_message: User message\n            model: Model to use (defaults to client's default_model)\n            max_tokens: Maximum tokens to generate\n            temperature: Sampling temperature\n\n        Yields:\n            Text chunks as they arrive\n\n        Returns:\n            Complete response text and usage info in the final iteration\n        \"\"\"\n        request = LLMRequest(\n            system_prompt=system_prompt,\n            user_message=user_message,\n            model=model or self.default_model,\n            max_tokens=max_tokens,\n            temperature=temperature,\n        )\n\n        client = await self._get_client()\n\n        try:\n            payload = request.to_payload()\n            payload[\"stream\"] = True  # Enable streaming\n\n            logger.debug(f\"LLM streaming request: model={request.model}\")\n\n            async with client.stream(\n                \"POST\",\n                self.proxy_url,\n                json=payload,\n                headers={\"Content-Type\": \"application/json\"},\n            ) as response:\n                # Check for rate limit\n                if response.status_code == 429:\n                    raise LLMRateLimitError(\"Rate limited by ZAI proxy\")\n\n                response.raise_for_status()\n\n                accumulated_text = []\n                input_tokens = 0\n                output_tokens = 0\n\n                # Process server-sent events\n                async for line in response.aiter_lines():\n                    if not line.strip():\n                        continue\n\n                    if line.startswith(\"data: \"):\n                        data_str = line[6:]  # Remove \"data: \" prefix\n\n                        if data_str == \"[DONE]\":\n                            break\n\n                        try:\n                            data = json.loads(data_str)\n\n                            # Handle ZAI proxy wrapping\n                            if \"result\" in data:\n                                data = data[\"result\"]\n\n                            # Extract delta from streaming event\n                            if data.get(\"type\") == \"content_block_delta\":\n                                delta = data.get(\"delta\", {})\n                                text = delta.get(\"text\", \"\")\n                                if text:\n                                    accumulated_text.append(text)\n                                    yield text\n\n                            # Extract final usage\n                            if data.get(\"type\") == \"message_stop\":\n                                usage = data.get(\"usage\", {})\n                                input_tokens = usage.get(\"input_tokens\", 0)\n                                output_tokens = usage.get(\"output_tokens\", 0)\n\n                        except json.JSONDecodeError as e:\n                            logger.warning(f\"Failed to parse streaming event: {e}\")\n\n                # Yield final result with usage info\n                full_text = \"\".join(accumulated_text)\n                logger.debug(f\"LLM streaming complete: input_tokens={input_tokens}, output_tokens={output_tokens}\")\n                yield {\n                    \"text\": full_text,\n                    \"input_tokens\": input_tokens,\n                    \"output_tokens\": output_tokens,\n                    \"finish_reason\": \"stop\",\n                }\n\n        except asyncio.TimeoutError as e:\n            raise LLMTimeoutError(f\"LLM streaming request timed out after {self.timeout}s\") from e\n        except httpx.TimeoutException as e:\n            raise LLMTimeoutError(f\"LLM streaming request timed out: {e}\") from e\n        except httpx.HTTPStatusError as e:\n            if e.response.status_code == 429:\n                raise LLMRateLimitError(\"Rate limited by ZAI proxy\") from e\n            raise LLMError(f\"LLM streaming request failed: {e.response.status_code}\") from e\n        except (LLMTimeoutError, LLMRateLimitError, LLMError):\n            raise\n        except Exception as e:\n            raise LLMError(f\"LLM streaming request failed: {e}\") from e\n\n\n# Global ZAI client instance\n_client: Optional[ZAIClient] = None\n\n\ndef get_zai_client(\n    proxy_url: str = ZAI_PROXY_URL,\n out\n            LLMRateLimitError: If rate limited\n            LLMError: For other errors\n        \"\"\"\n        client = await self._get_client()\n\n        try:\n            payload = LLMRequest(\n            system_prompt=system_prompt,\n            user_message=user_message,\n            model=model or self.default_model,\n            max_tokens=max_tokens,\n            temperature=temperature,\n        )\n        response = await self.call(request)\n        return response.content\n\n    async def call_streaming(\n        self,\n        system_prompt: str,\n user_message: User message\n            model: Model to use (defaults to client's default_model)\n            max_tokens: Maximum tokens to generate\n            temperature: Sampling temperature\n\n        system_prompt=system_prompt,\n            user_message=user_message,\n            model=model or self.default_model,\n            max_tokens=max_tokens,\n            temperature=temperature,\n        )\n\n        client = await self._get_client()\n\n        try:\n            payload = request.to_payload()\n            payload[\"stream\"] = True  # Enable streaming\n\n            logger.debug(f\"LLM streaming request: model={request.model}\")\n\n            async with client.stream(\n                \"POST\",\n                self.proxy_url,\n                json=payload,\n output_tokens = 0\n\n                # Process server-sent events\n                async for line in response.aiter_lines():\n                    if not line.strip():\n                        continue\n\n                    if line.startswith(\"data: \"):\n                        data_str = line[6:]  # Remove \"data: \" prefix\n\n                        if data_str == \"[DONE]\":\n streaming event: {e}\")\n\n                # Yield final result with usage info\n                full_text = \"\".join(accumulated_text)\n                logger.debug(f\"LLM streaming complete: input_tokens={input_tokens}, output_tokens={output_tokens}\")\n                yield {\n                    \"text\": full_text,\n                    \"input_tokens\": input_tokens,\n ZAI proxy response unwrapping (removes outer \"result\" field)\n2. Markdown code fence stripping (```json, ```, etc.)\n3. JSON parsing with clear error messages\n4. Graceful fallback for malformed responses\n\nThis utility is used across:\n- Intent Router (src/intent/router.py)\n- Synthesize Strand (src/synthesize/strand.py)\n- Self-Modification Agent (src/agents/self_modification.py)\n- Any other LLM interaction points\n\n## Error Handling Patterns\n\nWhen ParseLLMError is raised, choose recovery strategy based on context:\n\n### Corrective Retry Pattern (Router-style)\nUse when:\n- LLM call is early in pipeline (cheap to retry)\n- Failure cascades to downstream operations\n- Retrying doesn't require re-running expensive operations\n\nExample: Intent router retries once because malformed response affects all downstream processing.\n\nSee: src/intent/router.py classify_utterance()\n\n### Fallback Result Pattern (Synthesize-style)\nUse when:\n- LLM call is AFTER expensive operations (fetch, DB queries, etc.)\n- Data already obtained should not be discarded\n- User should see partial results in degraded-state UX\n\nExample: Synthesize strand returns fallback result because fetch operations already completed.\n\nSee: src/synthesize/strand.py synthesize()\n\n### Documentation\nSee: docs/error-handling-standardization.md for complete pattern comparison and examples.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport json\nfrom logging import getLogger\nfrom typing import Any, Dict, Optional, cast\n\nlogger = getLogger(__name__)\n\n\nclass ParseLLMError(Exception):\n    \"\"\"\n    Raised when LLM response parsing fails.\n\n    Attributes:\n        message: Human-readable error message\n        raw_response: The raw LLM response text that failed parsing (preserved for debugging)\n\n    The raw_response attribute is critical for debugging and degraded-state UX.\n    Always pass it when raising ParseLLMError so error handlers can include\n    response snippets in error events and logs.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, message: str, raw_response: Optional[str] = None):\n        self.raw_response = raw_response\n        super().__init__(message)\n\n\ndef strip_markdown_fences(raw: str) -> str:\n    \"\"\"\n    Strip markdown code fences from a response using fast manual string splitting.\n\n    Handles:\n    - ```json ... ```\n    - ``` ... ```\n    - ` ... ` (rare but possible)\n\n    Performance: Uses manual string splitting which is 7-179x faster than regex-based\n    alternatives (see benchmarking in notes/adc-3e5gg.md). This is the optimal approach\n    for hot-path LLM response parsing.\n\n    Args:\n        raw: Raw response text that may contain markdown fences\n\n    Returns:\n        Text with markdown fences removed\n\n    Examples:\n        >>> strip_markdown_fences('```json\\\\n{\"a\": 1}\\\\n```')\n        '{\"a\": 1}'\n        >>> strip_markdown_fences('```\\\\n{\"a\": 1}\\\\n```')\n        '{\"a\": 1}'\n        >>> strip_markdown_fences('{\"a\": 1}')\n        '{\"a\": 1}'\n    \"\"\"\n    # Early return for empty/whitespace strings\n    if not raw or not raw.strip():\n        return raw\n\n    # Optimized: single strip at start, avoid redundant operations\n    text = raw.strip()\n\n    # Fast manual fence stripping (7-179x faster than regex)\n    # Pattern: ```optional_lang\\n content \\n```\n    if text.startswith(\"```\"):\n        # Split after first newline to skip opening fence line\n        text = text.split(\"\\n\", 1)[-1]\n        # Remove closing fence and any trailing whitespace\n        text = text.rsplit(\"```\", 1)[0].strip()\n\n    return text\n\n\ndef unwrap_zai_response(response_data: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Unwrap ZAI proxy response structure.\n\n    The ZAI proxy wraps Anthropic responses under a \"result\" key.\n    This function extracts the inner payload if present.\n\n    Args:\n        response_data: Raw response dict from ZAI proxy\n\n    Returns:\n        Unwrapped response dict (or original if no wrapping detected)\n\n    Examples:\n        >>> unwrap_zai_response({\"result\": {\"content\": [...]}})\n        {\"content\": [...]}\n        >>> unwrap_zai_response({\"content\": [...]})\n        {\"content\": [...]}\n    \"\"\"\n    # Check if response has \"result\" wrapper (ZAI proxy pattern)\n    if \"result\" in response_data and isinstance(response_data[\"result\"], dict):\n        inner = response_data[\"result\"]\n        logger.debug(\"Unwrapped ZAI proxy response (removed 'result' field)\")\n        return inner\n\n    return response_data\n\n\ndef extract_text_from_response(payload: Dict[str, Any]) -> str:\n    \"\"\"\n    Extract text content from an Anthropic-style response payload.\n\n    Args:\n        payload: Response payload with \"content\" array\n\n    Returns:\n        Extracted text content\n\n    Raises:\n        ParseLLMError: If content cannot be extracted\n    \"\"\"\n    content = payload.get(\"content\", [])\n\n    if content and isinstance(content, list) and len(content) > 0:\n        text = cast(str, content[0].get(\"text\", \"\"))\n        if text:\n            return text\n\n    # Fallback: try to stringify the content\n    logger.warning(\"Could not extract text from content array, using fallback\")\n    return str(content)\n\n\ndef parse_llm_response(\n    raw_text: str,\n    *,\n    strip_fences: bool = True,\n    expect_json: bool = True,\n) -> Any:\n    \"\"\"\n    Parse an LLM text response, optionally stripping fences and parsing JSON.\n\n    This is the main entry point for parsing LLM responses across the codebase.\n    It handles the common pattern of:\n    1. Stripping markdown code fences (```json ... ```)\n    2. Parsing JSON (when expect_json=True)\n    3. Providing clear error messages\n\n    Performance: Optimized with fast manual fence stripping (7-179x faster than\n    regex) and early returns on empty inputs. Operations are sequential but efficient.\n\n    Args:\n        raw_text: Raw response text from LLM\n        strip_fences: Whether to strip markdown code fences (default: True)\n        expect_json: Whether to parse as JSON (default: True)\n\n    Returns:\n        Parsed JSON object if expect_json=True, otherwise cleaned text\n\n    Raises:\n        ParseLLMError: If parsing fails\n\n    Examples:\n        >>> parse_llm_response('```json\\\\n{\"a\": 1}\\\\n```')\n        {'a': 1}\n        >>> parse_llm_response('```json\\\\n{\"a\": 1}\\\\n```', expect_json=False)\n        '{\"a\": 1}'\n    \"\"\"\n    # Early return for empty input\n    if not raw_text or not raw_text.strip():\n        if expect_json:\n            raise ParseLLMError(\n                \"Empty response provided\",\n                raw_response=raw_text,\n            )\n        return raw_text\n\n    try:\n        # Step 1: Strip markdown fences if present\n        if strip_fences:\n            text = strip_markdown_fences(raw_text)\n        else:\n            text = raw_text.strip()\n\n        # Step 2: Parse JSON if expected\n        if expect_json:\n            try:\n                return json.loads(text)\n            except json.JSONDecodeError as e:\n                # Provide helpful error message with snippet\n                snippet = text[:200] if text else \"(empty)\"\n                raise ParseLLMError(\n                    f\"Failed to parse JSON: {e}\\nResponse snippet: {snippet}...\",\n                    raw_response=raw_text,\n                ) from e\n\n        return text\n\n    except ParseLLMError:\n        # Re-raise ParseLLMError without wrapping\n        raise\n    except Exception as e:\n        raise ParseLLMError(\n            f\"Unexpected error parsing response: {e}\",\n            raw_response=raw_text,\n        ) from e\n\n\ndef parse_zai_proxy_response(\n    response_dict: Dict[str, Any],\n    *,\n    extract_text: bool = True,\n    strip_fences: bool = True,\n    expect_json: bool = True,\n) -> Any:\n    \"\"\"\n    Parse a complete ZAI proxy response from HTTP.\n\n    This handles the full flow:\n    1. Unwrap ZAI proxy's \"result\" field\n    2. Extract text from Anthropic content array\n    3. Strip markdown fences\n    4. Parse JSON if expected\n\n    Args:\n        response_dict: Raw response dict from httpx.Response.json()\n        extract_text: Whether to extract text from content array (default: True)\n        strip_fences: Whether to strip markdown fences (default: True)\n        expect_json: Whether to parse as JSON (default: True)\n\n    Returns:\n        Parsed JSON object or text depending on expect_json\n\n    Raises:\n        ParseLLMError: If parsing fails\n\n    Examples:\n        >>> response = {\"result\": {\"content\": [{\"text\": '```json\\\\n{\"a\": 1}\\\\n```'}]}}\n        >>> parse_zai_proxy_response(response)\n        {'a': 1}\n    \"\"\"\n    try:\n        # Step 1: Unwrap ZAI proxy response\n        unwrapped = unwrap_zai_response(response_dict)\n\n        # Step 2: Extract text from content structure\n        if extract_text:\n            text = extract_text_from_response(unwrapped)\n        else:\n            # Return the unwrapped payload as-is\n            return unwrapped\n\n        # Step 3 & 4: Strip fences and parse JSON using the main parser\n        return parse_llm_response(\n            text,\n            strip_fences=strip_fences,\n            expect_json=expect_json,\n        )\n\n    except ParseLLMError:\n        raise\n    except Exception as e:\n        raise ParseLLMError(\n            f\"Failed to parse ZAI proxy response: {e}\",\n            raw_response=str(response_dict)[:500],\n        ) from e\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":43,"oldLines":7,"newStart":43,"newLines":7,"lines":[" "," import json"," from logging import getLogger","-from typing import Any, Dict, Optional, cast","+from typing import Any, Dict, Optional, Union, cast"," "," logger = getLogger(__name__)"," "]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}

ZAI proxy response unwrapping (removes outer \"result\" field)\n2. Markdown code fence stripping (```json, ```, etc.)\n3. JSON parsing with clear error messages\n4. Graceful fallback for malformed responses\n\nThis utility is used across:\n- Intent Router (src/intent/router.py)\n- Synthesize Strand (src/synthesize/strand.py)\n- Self-Modification Agent (src/agents/self_modification.py)\n- Any other LLM interaction points\n\n## Error Handling Patterns\n\nWhen ParseLLMError is raised, choose recovery strategy based on context:\n\n### Corrective Retry Pattern (Router-style)\nUse when:\n- LLM call is early in pipeline (cheap to retry)\n- Failure cascades to downstream operations\n- Retrying doesn't require re-running expensive operations\n\nExample: Intent router retries once because malformed response affects all downstream processing.\n\nSee: src/intent/router.py classify_utterance()\n\n### Fallback Result Pattern (Synthesize-style)\nUse when:\n- LLM call is AFTER expensive operations (fetch, DB queries, etc.)\n- Data already obtained should not be discarded\n- User should see partial results in degraded-state UX\n\nExample: Synthesize strand returns fallback result because fetch operations already completed.\n\nSee: src/synthesize/strand.py synthesize()\n\n### Documentation\nSee: docs/error-handling-standardization.md for complete pattern comparison and examples.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport json\nfrom logging import getLogger\nfrom typing import Any, Dict, Optional, Union, cast\n\nlogger = getLogger(__name__)\n\n\nclass ParseLLMError(Exception):\n    \"\"\"\n    Raised when LLM response parsing fails.\n\n    Attributes:\n        message: Human-readable error message\n        raw_response: The raw LLM response text that failed parsing (preserved for debugging)\n\n    The raw_response attribute is critical for debugging and degraded-state UX.\n    Always pass it when raising ParseLLMError so error handlers can include\n    response snippets in error events and logs.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, message: str, raw_response: Optional[str] = None):\n        self.raw_response = raw_response\n        super().__init__(message)\n\n\ndef strip_markdown_fences(raw: str) -> str:\n    \"\"\"\n    Strip markdown code fences from a response using fast manual string splitting.\n\n    Handles:\n    - ```json ... ```\n    - ``` ... ```\n    - ` ... ` (rare but possible)\n\n    Performance: Uses manual string splitting which is 7-179x faster than regex-based\n    alternatives (see benchmarking in notes/adc-3e5gg.md). This is the optimal approach\n    for hot-path LLM response parsing.\n\n    Args:\n        raw: Raw response text that may contain markdown fences\n\n    Returns:\n        Text with markdown fences removed\n\n    Examples:\n        >>> strip_markdown_fences('```json\\\\n{\"a\": 1}\\\\n```')\n        '{\"a\": 1}'\n        >>> strip_markdown_fences('```\\\\n{\"a\": 1}\\\\n```')\n        '{\"a\": 1}'\n        >>> strip_markdown_fences('{\"a\": 1}')\n        '{\"a\": 1}'\n    \"\"\"\n    # Early return for empty/whitespace strings\n    if not raw or not raw.strip():\n        return raw\n\n    # Optimized: single strip at start, avoid redundant operations\n    text = raw.strip()\n\n    # Fast manual fence stripping (7-179x faster than regex)\n    # Pattern: ```optional_lang\\n content \\n```\n    if text.startswith(\"```\"):\n        # Split after first newline to skip opening fence line\n        text = text.split(\"\\n\", 1)[-1]\n        # Remove closing fence and any trailing whitespace\n        text = text.rsplit(\"```\", 1)[0].strip()\n\n    return text\n\n\ndef unwrap_zai_response(response_data: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Unwrap ZAI proxy response structure.\n\n    The ZAI proxy wraps Anthropic responses under a \"result\" key.\n    This function extracts the inner payload if present.\n\n    Args:\n        response_data: Raw response dict from ZAI proxy\n\n    Returns:\n        Unwrapped response dict (or original if no wrapping detected)\n\n    Examples:\n        >>> unwrap_zai_response({\"result\": {\"content\": [...]}})\n        {\"content\": [...]}\n        >>> unwrap_zai_response({\"content\": [...]})\n        {\"content\": [...]}\n    \"\"\"\n    # Check if response has \"result\" wrapper (ZAI proxy pattern)\n    if \"result\" in response_data and isinstance(response_data[\"result\"], dict):\n        inner = response_data[\"result\"]\n        logger.debug(\"Unwrapped ZAI proxy response (removed 'result' field)\")\n        return inner\n\n    return response_data\n\n\ndef extract_text_from_response(payload: Dict[str, Any]) -> str:\n    \"\"\"\n    Extract text content from an Anthropic-style response payload.\n\n    Args:\n        payload: Response payload with \"content\" array\n\n    Returns:\n        Extracted text content\n\n    Raises:\n        ParseLLMError: If content cannot be extracted\n    \"\"\"\n    content = payload.get(\"content\", [])\n\n    if content and isinstance(content, list) and len(content) > 0:\n        text = cast(str, content[0].get(\"text\", \"\"))\n        if text:\n            return text\n\n    # Fallback: try to stringify the content\n    logger.warning(\"Could not extract text from content array, using fallback\")\n    return str(content)\n\n\ndef parse_llm_response(\n    raw_text: str,\n    *,\n    strip_fences: bool = True,\n    expect_json: bool = True,\n) -> Any:\n    \"\"\"\n    Parse an LLM text response, optionally stripping fences and parsing JSON.\n\n    This is the main entry point for parsing LLM responses across the codebase.\n    It handles the common pattern of:\n    1. Stripping markdown code fences (```json ... ```)\n    2. Parsing JSON (when expect_json=True)\n    3. Providing clear error messages\n\n    Performance: Optimized with fast manual fence stripping (7-179x faster than\n    regex) and early returns on empty inputs. Operations are sequential but efficient.\n\n    Args:\n        raw_text: Raw response text from LLM\n        strip_fences: Whether to strip markdown code fences (default: True)\n        expect_json: Whether to parse as JSON (default: True)\n\n    Returns:\n        Parsed JSON object if expect_json=True, otherwise cleaned text\n\n    Raises:\n        ParseLLMError: If parsing fails\n\n    Examples:\n        >>> parse_llm_response('```json\\\\n{\"a\": 1}\\\\n```')\n        {'a': 1}\n        >>> parse_llm_response('```json\\\\n{\"a\": 1}\\\\n```', expect_json=False)\n        '{\"a\": 1}'\n    \"\"\"\n    # Early return for empty input\n    if not raw_text or not raw_text.strip():\n        if expect_json:\n            raise ParseLLMError(\n                \"Empty response provided\",\n                raw_response=raw_text,\n            )\n        return raw_text\n\n    try:\n        # Step 1: Strip markdown fences if present\n        if strip_fences:\n            text = strip_markdown_fences(raw_text)\n        else:\n            text = raw_text.strip()\n\n        # Step 2: Parse JSON if expected\n        if expect_json:\n            try:\n                return json.loads(text)\n            except json.JSONDecodeError as e:\n                # Provide helpful error message with snippet\n                snippet = text[:200] if text else \"(empty)\"\n                raise ParseLLMError(\n                    f\"Failed to parse JSON: {e}\\nResponse snippet: {snippet}...\",\n                    raw_response=raw_text,\n                ) from e\n\n        return text\n\n    except ParseLLMError:\n        # Re-raise ParseLLMError without wrapping\n        raise\n    except Exception as e:\n        raise ParseLLMError(\n            f\"Unexpected error parsing response: {e}\",\n            raw_response=raw_text,\n        ) from e\n\n\ndef parse_zai_proxy_response(\n    response_dict: Dict[str, Any],\n    *,\n    extract_text: bool = True,\n    strip_fences: bool = True,\n    expect_json: bool = True,\n) -> Any:\n    \"\"\"\n    Parse a complete ZAI proxy response from HTTP.\n\n    This handles the full flow:\n    1. Unwrap ZAI proxy's \"result\" field\n    2. Extract text from Anthropic content array\n    3. Strip markdown fences\n    4. Parse JSON if expected\n\n    Args:\n        response_dict: Raw response dict from httpx.Response.json()\n        extract_text: Whether to extract text from content array (default: True)\n        strip_fences: Whether to strip markdown fences (default: True)\n        expect_json: Whether to parse as JSON (default: True)\n\n    Returns:\n        Parsed JSON object or text depending on expect_json\n\n    Raises:\n        ParseLLMError: If parsing fails\n\n    Examples:\n        >>> response = {\"result\": {\"content\": [{\"text\": '```json\\\\n{\"a\": 1}\\\\n```'}]}}\n        >>> parse_zai_proxy_response(response)\n        {'a': 1}\n    \"\"\"\n    try:\n        # Step 1: Unwrap ZAI proxy response\n        unwrapped = unwrap_zai_response(response_dict)\n\n        # Step 2: Extract text from content structure\n        if extract_text:\n            text = extract_text_from_response(unwrapped)\n        else:\n            # Return the unwrapped payload as-is\n            return unwrapped\n\n        # Step 3 & 4: Strip fences and parse JSON using the main parser\n        return parse_llm_response(\n            text,\n            strip_fences=strip_fences,\n            expect_json=expect_json,\n        )\n\n    except ParseLLMError:\n        raise\n    except Exception as e:\n        raise ParseLLMError(\n            f\"Failed to parse ZAI proxy response: {e}\",\n            raw_response=str(response_dict)[:500],\n        ) from e\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":169,"oldLines":7,"newStart":169,"newLines":7,"lines":["     *,","     strip_fences: bool = True,","     expect_json: bool = True,","-) -> Any:","+) -> Union[str, Dict[str, Any]]:","     \"\"\"","     Parse an LLM text response, optionally stripping fences and parsing JSON."," "]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}

ZAI proxy response unwrapping (removes outer \"result\" field)\n2. Markdown code fence stripping (```json, ```, etc.)\n3. JSON parsing with clear error messages\n4. Graceful fallback for malformed responses\n\nThis utility is used across:\n- Intent Router (src/intent/router.py)\n- Synthesize Strand (src/synthesize/strand.py)\n- Self-Modification Agent (src/agents/self_modification.py)\n- Any other LLM interaction points\n\n## Error Handling Patterns\n\nWhen ParseLLMError is raised, choose recovery strategy based on context:\n\n### Corrective Retry Pattern (Router-style)\nUse when:\n- LLM call is early in pipeline (cheap to retry)\n- Failure cascades to downstream operations\n- Retrying doesn't require re-running expensive operations\n\nExample: Intent router retries once because malformed response affects all downstream processing.\n\nSee: src/intent/router.py classify_utterance()\n\n### Fallback Result Pattern (Synthesize-style)\nUse when:\n- LLM call is AFTER expensive operations (fetch, DB queries, etc.)\n- Data already obtained should not be discarded\n- User should see partial results in degraded-state UX\n\nExample: Synthesize strand returns fallback result because fetch operations already completed.\n\nSee: src/synthesize/strand.py synthesize()\n\n### Documentation\nSee: docs/error-handling-standardization.md for complete pattern comparison and examples.\n\"\"\"\n\nimport json\nfrom logging import getLogger\nfrom typing import Any, Dict, Optional, Union, cast\n\nlogger = getLogger(__name__)\n\n\nclass ParseLLMError(Exception):\n    \"\"\"\n    Raised when LLM response parsing fails.\n\n    Attributes:\n        message: Human-readable error message\n        raw_response: The raw LLM response text that failed parsing (preserved for debugging)\n\n    The raw_response attribute is critical for debugging and degraded-state UX.\n    Always pass it when raising ParseLLMError so error handlers can include\n    response snippets in error events and logs.\n    \"\"\"\n\n    def __init__(self, message: str, raw_response: Optional[str] = None):\n        self.raw_response = raw_response\n        super().__init__(message)\n\n\ndef strip_markdown_fences(raw: str) -> str:\n    \"\"\"\n    Strip markdown code fences from a response using fast manual string splitting.\n\n    Handles:\n    - ```json ... ```\n    - ``` ... ```\n    - ` ... ` (rare but possible)\n\n    Performance: Uses manual string splitting which is 7-179x faster than regex-based\n    alternatives (see benchmarking in notes/adc-3e5gg.md). This is the optimal approach\n    for hot-path LLM response parsing.\n\n    Args:\n        raw: Raw response text that may contain markdown fences\n\n    Returns:\n        Text with markdown fences removed\n\n    Examples:\n        >>> strip_markdown_fences('```json\\\\n{\"a\": 1}\\\\n```')\n        '{\"a\": 1}'\n        >>> strip_markdown_fences('```\\\\n{\"a\": 1}\\\\n```')\n        '{\"a\": 1}'\n        >>> strip_markdown_fences('{\"a\": 1}')\n        '{\"a\": 1}'\n    \"\"\"\n    # Early return for empty/whitespace strings\n    if not raw or not raw.strip():\n        return raw\n\n    # Optimized: single strip at start, avoid redundant operations\n    text = raw.strip()\n\n    # Fast manual fence stripping (7-179x faster than regex)\n    # Pattern: ```optional_lang\\n content \\n```\n    if text.startswith(\"```\"):\n        # Split after first newline to skip opening fence line\n        text = text.split(\"\\n\", 1)[-1]\n        # Remove closing fence and any trailing whitespace\n        text = text.rsplit(\"```\", 1)[0].strip()\n\n    return text\n\n\ndef unwrap_zai_response(response_data: Dict[str, Any]) -> Dict[str, Any]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Unwrap ZAI proxy response structure.\n\n    The ZAI proxy wraps Anthropic responses under a \"result\" key.\n    This function extracts the inner payload if present.\n\n    Args:\n        response_data: Raw response dict from ZAI proxy\n\n    Returns:\n        Unwrapped response dict (or original if no wrapping detected)\n\n    Examples:\n        >>> unwrap_zai_response({\"result\": {\"content\": [...]}})\n        {\"content\": [...]}\n        >>> unwrap_zai_response({\"content\": [...]})\n        {\"content\": [...]}\n    \"\"\"\n    # Check if response has \"result\" wrapper (ZAI proxy pattern)\n    if \"result\" in response_data and isinstance(response_data[\"result\"], dict):\n        inner = response_data[\"result\"]\n        logger.debug(\"Unwrapped ZAI proxy response (removed 'result' field)\")\n        return inner\n\n    return response_data\n\n\ndef extract_text_from_response(payload: Dict[str, Any]) -> str:\n    \"\"\"\n    Extract text content from an Anthropic-style response payload.\n\n    Args:\n        payload: Response payload with \"content\" array\n\n    Returns:\n        Extracted text content\n\n    Raises:\n        ParseLLMError: If content cannot be extracted\n    \"\"\"\n    content = payload.get(\"content\", [])\n\n    if content and isinstance(content, list) and len(content) > 0:\n        text = cast(str, content[0].get(\"text\", \"\"))\n        if text:\n            return text\n\n    # Fallback: try to stringify the content\n    logger.warning(\"Could not extract text from content array, using fallback\")\n    return str(content)\n\n\ndef parse_llm_response(\n    raw_text: str,\n    *,\n    strip_fences: bool = True,\n    expect_json: bool = True,\n) -> Union[str, Dict[str, Any]]:\n    \"\"\"\n    Parse an LLM text response, optionally stripping fences and parsing JSON.\n\n    This is the main entry point for parsing LLM responses across the codebase.\n    It handles the common pattern of:\n    1. Stripping markdown code fences (```json ... ```)\n    2. Parsing JSON (when expect_json=True)\n    3. Providing clear error messages\n\n    Performance: Optimized with fast manual fence stripping (7-179x faster than\n    regex) and early returns on empty inputs. Operations are sequential but efficient.\n\n    Args:\n        raw_text: Raw response text from LLM\n        strip_fences: Whether to strip markdown code fences (default: True)\n        expect_json: Whether to parse as JSON (default: True)\n\n    Returns:\n        Parsed JSON object if expect_json=True, otherwise cleaned text\n\n    Raises:\n        ParseLLMError: If parsing fails\n\n    Examples:\n        >>> parse_llm_response('```json\\\\n{\"a\": 1}\\\\n```')\n        {'a': 1}\n        >>> parse_llm_response('```json\\\\n{\"a\": 1}\\\\n```', expect_json=False)\n        '{\"a\": 1}'\n    \"\"\"\n    # Early return for empty input\n    if not raw_text or not raw_text.strip():\n        if expect_json:\n            raise ParseLLMError(\n                \"Empty response provided\",\n                raw_response=raw_text,\n            )\n        return raw_text\n\n    try:\n        # Step 1: Strip markdown fences if present\n        if strip_fences:\n            text = strip_markdown_fences(raw_text)\n        else:\n            text = raw_text.strip()\n\n        # Step 2: Parse JSON if expected\n        if expect_json:\n            try:\n                return json.loads(text)\n            except json.JSONDecodeError as e:\n                # Provide helpful error message with snippet\n                snippet = text[:200] if text else \"(empty)\"\n                raise ParseLLMError(\n                    f\"Failed to parse JSON: {e}\\nResponse snippet: {snippet}...\",\n                    raw_response=raw_text,\n                ) from e\n\n        return text\n\n    except ParseLLMError:\n        # Re-raise ParseLLMError without wrapping\n        raise\n    except Exception as e:\n        raise ParseLLMError(\n            f\"Unexpected error parsing response: {e}\",\n            raw_response=raw_text,\n        ) from e\n\n\ndef parse_zai_proxy_response(\n    response_dict: Dict[str, Any],\n    *,\n    extract_text: bool = True,\n    strip_fences: bool = True,\n    expect_json: bool = True,\n) -> Any:\n    \"\"\"\n    Parse a complete ZAI proxy response from HTTP.\n\n    This handles the full flow:\n    1. Unwrap ZAI proxy's \"result\" field\n    2. Extract text from Anthropic content array\n    3. Strip markdown fences\n    4. Parse JSON if expected\n\n    Args:\n        response_dict: Raw response dict from httpx.Response.json()\n        extract_text: Whether to extract text from content array (default: True)\n        strip_fences: Whether to strip markdown fences (default: True)\n        expect_json: Whether to parse as JSON (default: True)\n\n    Returns:\n        Parsed JSON object or text depending on expect_json\n\n    Raises:\n        ParseLLMError: If parsing fails\n\n    Examples:\n        >>> response = {\"result\": {\"content\": [{\"text\": '```json\\\\n{\"a\": 1}\\\\n```'}]}}\n        >>> parse_zai_proxy_response(response)\n        {'a': 1}\n    \"\"\"\n    try:\n        # Step 1: Unwrap ZAI proxy response\n        unwrapped = unwrap_zai_response(response_dict)\n\n        # Step 2: Extract text from content structure\n        if extract_text:\n            text = extract_text_from_response(unwrapped)\n        else:\n            # Return the unwrapped payload as-is\n            return unwrapped\n\n        # Step 3 & 4: Strip fences and parse JSON using the main parser\n        return parse_llm_response(\n            text,\n            strip_fences=strip_fences,\n            expect_json=expect_json,\n        )\n\n    except ParseLLMError:\n        raise\n    except Exception as e:\n        raise ParseLLMError(\n            f\"Failed to parse ZAI proxy response: {e}\",\n            raw_response=str(response_dict)[:500],\n        ) from e\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":245,"oldLines":7,"newStart":245,"newLines":7,"lines":["     extract_text: bool = True,","     strip_fences: bool = True,","     expect_json: bool = True,","-) -> Any:","+) -> Union[str, Dict[str, Any]]:","     \"\"\"","     Parse a complete ZAI proxy response from HTTP."," "]}],"userModified":false,"replaceAll":false}}
